user.title: Connect to Celsiview
user.description: The Celsiview API lives at https://api.celsiview.se (this is the pre-filled default and you almost never need to change it). API keys are managed from the web app at https://app.celsiview.se/api/keys. If your key has client_secret_required enabled, also provide the client secret.

select.title: Select sensors
select.description: Found {count} locations on your account. Pick the ones you want to import as Home Assistant sensors. You can change this later from the integration options.

error.invalid_auth: The application key (or client secret) was rejected by Celsiview.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach Celsiview. Check the base URL and your network.
error.unknown: Unexpected error; see Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This Celsiview account is already configured.
abort.no_locations: No locations were returned by the API. Grant the API user access to at least one location and try again.

init.title: Celsiview options
init.description: Select which locations to keep imported and how often to poll the Celsiview API. {count} locations are available.

data.backfill_states: Backfill every sample into the states table (advanced, unsupported)

data_description.backfill_states: If enabled, every Celsiview sample is written directly into Home Assistant's recorder states table at its on-device timestamp, so the standard History tab shows full sample-rate detail instead of stepping at upload cadence. This bypasses Home Assistant's public API and writes to internal recorder tables. It can break across HA versions if the recorder schema changes, and may cause excessive recorder DB growth on long backfills. Hourly long-term statistics are imported regardless of this setting; leave this off unless you specifically want sample-resolution state history.

error.invalid_auth: The stored credentials are no longer accepted by Celsiview. Remove and re-add the integration.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach Celsiview. Check the base URL and your network.
